summary: Gruber announces that Mac OS X Leopard will ship on October 26 and promotes pre-ordering through his Amazon affiliate links. He notes Amazon matches Apple's pricing with free shipping, and highlights Amazon's pre-order price guarantee, which already resulted in a $20 price drop within a day. The post is a straightforward affiliate pitch with Gruber's characteristic transparency about the kickback he receives.
key_points: Mac OS X Leopard confirmed to ship October 26, 2007, at $129 single or $199 family pack
Amazon matched Apple's price with free shipping and a pre-order price guarantee, quickly dropping the single-license price by $20
Gruber is transparent about earning a 7.5 percent Amazon affiliate kickback on purchases
Macs purchased after October 1 qualify for Leopard at just a $10 shipping fee
money_quotes: So the cat's out of the bag: Mac OS X 10.5, a.k.a. Leopard, will indeed ship on October 26.
I.e., you get a good price and free shipping, and I get about $10.
One day later and Amazon has already cut $20 off the price of the single-license version and $10 off the price of the family pack.
key_insight: A brief, transparent affiliate pitch for Leopard pre-orders that reflects Gruber's direct relationship with his audience and his willingness to be upfront about monetization.
